title: Phase 16: PSM 0x0030 j2dsrv 47FCC338 record decode
objective: 把 PSM type 0x0030 decoder 从错误的 GArc2d 假设改写为基于真实 j2dsrv.dll CLSID 47FCC338 复合 record 的 conservative typed decoder
evidence: Goal package scaffolded from Phase 14 §6.1 trigger + 3 rounds of probe_garc2d_packed_bytes + IDA radsrvitem.dll PersistTypeTable lookup (port 13346); CLSID j2dsrv.dll location confirmed via PowerShell binary grep across dlls/.

summary: Cross-fixture 98 raw 0x0030 hits (current decoder filters to 48 via wrong axis_a.y constraint). Payload +0..15 = center.xy (verified). Payload +16..23 = unknown (39% byte-eq with center.x, 55% not). Payload +24..31 = discrete angles {0, π/2, 3π/2, 2π} (likely rotation_angle or sweep_extent). Payload +32..63 = packed PSM references to igLine2d / igTextBox / igSymbol2d / GraphicGroup / 0x0010 sub-records. Tail contains length-prefixed UTF-16LE plant tag (e.g. A3-FA060201), companion coords, 1.0 marker, more packed references. PSM table entry[48] in radsrvitem.dll dword_5667B068 confirms CLSID = {47FCC338-2D0F-11D0-A1FF-080036A1CF02}. CLSID family 47FCC330..47FCC33E (PSM type 0x29..0x35) all from j2dsrv.dll (RAD 2D Service, FileVersion 09.00.00.0138).

note: Phase 16 scope locked, IDA loading of j2dsrv.dll pending user action.

summary: Added 3 new analyses to probe: +24..31 by btf bucket (rotation_angle hypothesis confirmed: btf=224/225 → 100% zero, btf=145 → π/2 in DWG fixtures; values集中在 {0, π/2, 3π/2}); tail head signature by btf bucket (plant tag presence is record-level option, not btf-determined); cross-record oid references using build_known_oid_table (records_with_payload_hit=100% across all 4 fixtures; payload+038 / +042 / +050 are stable u32 reference_oid fields with 100% hit rate; referenced PSM type distribution heavy on 0x0010 sub-record + 0x00FA GraphicGroup + igTextBox + igSymbol2d). Sample DWG-0202 oid=1: refs igTextBox(336) + GraphicGroup(4756) + 0x0010(65538) + plant tag 'A3-FA060201'.

Documented probe v5 findings: rotation_angle vs sweep_extent decision (rotation wins), stable reference fields (+38 / +42 / +50 = u32 oid references with 100% hit rate), referenced PSM type distribution, full sample interpretation of DWG-0202 oid=1 as instrument with tag/label/group/sub-record chain. Lists 11 remaining open questions for Slice B/IDA.

J2DSrv.dll loaded into IDA MCP port 13347 (pid=124080, idb=E:\reverse\pid\J2DSrv.dll.i64, imagebase=0x10000000, hexrays_ready). CLSID 47FCC338 located at RVA 0x100145F8 with 1 code xref from sub_10001AB0 (J2DSrv application init via JCoCreateInstance, consumer not implementer; stored to this+1416). J2DSrv.dll exports DllGetClassObject at 0x10009D20 (may register some CLSIDs as implementer). Next slice: decompile DllGetClassObject + find 47FCC338's true implementing DLL (CLSID present in 73 .dll/.ocx/.dax/.dcx files across E:\reverse\pid\).

reason: User day-end; IDA Slice A captured (j2dsrv loaded + xref located + consumer/implementer distinction); next action: continue Slice A by decompiling DllGetClassObject on port 13347, find ClassFactory dll for 47FCC338, load it to IDA, then proceed to Slice B (reverse vtable Save/Load).

summary: Decompiled J2DSrv DllGetClassObject (0x10009D20): supports ONLY CLSID {4B516832-48FB-11CE-A4C3-080036 01D975} = DNameNode. Returns CLASS_E_CLASSNOTAVAILABLE for all other CLSIDs. So J2DSrv.dll is NOT the implementer of 47FCC338. Also verified CLSID 47FCC338 is NOT registered in Windows OLE (HKLM/HKCU CLSID hive returns NOT_FOUND). SmartSketch / RAD uses custom JCoCreateInstance dispatch instead, imported from JUTIL.dll (E:\reverse\pid\JUTIL.dll, FileVersion 07.00.00.0107, Product RAD). PowerShell binary grep confirms JUTIL.dll contains CLSID 47FCC338 @ file offset 0x35690 AND a 'DllGetClassObject' ASCII string @ 0x26BAC, making it a strong implementer candidate.

blocker: JUTIL.dll IDA instance not yet registered (Start-Process launched IDA pid=12184 to load E:\reverse\pid\JUTIL.dll, but list_instances does not show it after 55s wait); likely waiting on user click-through of IDA initial dialogs (load .pdb? / create new database?)
next_action: After JUTIL.dll registers to a new MCP port (≥ 13348), select_instance to it, decompile DllGetClassObject and JCoCreateInstance impl, locate 47FCC338's ClassFactory + its target dll (could be JUTIL itself or further indirection). Then load implementer dll and proceed to Slice B (reverse Save/Load vtable for 47FCC338's class to obtain authoritative field table).

artifact: PowerShell static byte analysis of JUTIL.dll @ 0x35680..0x35780
summary: Bypassed IDA (Loading still hung) via direct PowerShell hex dump. JUTIL.dll contains a RAD framework CLSID registry table at file offset 0x35680, 64 bytes per entry with layout: +0..15 zero/flags, +16..31 CLSID (16 bytes), +32..47 implementing dll name (16 bytes, null-terminated), +48..63 friendly class name (16 bytes, null-terminated). Decoded 3 entries spanning the JSL family: 47FCC338→style.dll "JSL Override Style"; 47FCC339→style.dll "JSL Offset Line Generator"; 47FCC33A→style.dll "JSL Bitmap Style". CONCLUSIVE: PSM type 0x0030 = JSL Override Style class implemented in style.dll (Product=RAD, FileVersion 07.00.00.0108, 526336 bytes). Family 47FCC330..47FCC33E all live in style.dll → PSM type code 0x29..0x35 are all RAD JSL Style records.

CLSID name 'JSL Override Style' is RAD developer terminology. In SmartPlant fixtures, this PSM record carries instrument anchor + plant tag + reference linkage (cross-record probe v5 evidence). Two hypotheses: (a) SmartPlant overloads JSL Style as generic record container; or (b) SmartPlant subclasses JSL Override Style and the IJPersist Save/Load writes instrument-specific fields. Cannot disambiguate without reading style.dll Save/Load. Next slice loads style.dll to IDA.

Started IDA loading style.dll. CLSID 47FCC338 confirmed at style.dll file offset 0x00065F64 (matches earlier PowerShell scan). Awaiting MCP registration; next session can select_instance to it and decompile DllGetClassObject + 47FCC338 ClassFactory + JSL Override Style Save/Load vtable slot for authoritative field table.

reason: Day-end pause; IDA Slice A fully completes the discovery chain: radsrvitem PSM table → CLSID 47FCC338 = JSL Override Style → implementer style.dll. Probe v5 fully decoded fixture byte layout to stable reference oid fields + rotation angle. Phase 16 goal-package complete. Next session: wait for style.dll IDA MCP registration (or user re-launch), decompile JSL Override Style Save/Load vtable slot, write authoritative field table to docs/analysis/2026-05-1?-jsl-override-style-fields.md, proceed to Slice C (DTO/decoder rename with user).

summary: style.dll loaded into IDA MCP port 13348 (pid=57844, idb=E:\reverse\pid\style.dll.i64, imagebase=0x10000000, hexrays_ready). CLSID 47FCC338 located at RVA 0x10066B64. xrefs from: DllGetClassObject (0x100047B0, 0x905 bytes, big if-else chain over CLSIDs), HCreateOverrideStyle (0x10043500, 0x362 bytes), 3 overloaded HGetOverrideStyle (with explicit C++ types: tagStyleOverride / tagStyleSimpleDashTypeData / IJStyleDashTypeRender). DllGetClassObject branch for 47FCC338 calls sub_10001600 which sets vtable to ClassFactory<JStyleOverride>::vftable (??_7?$ClassFactory@VJStyleOverride@@@@6B@ @ 0x100697D4). CONFIRMED: real C++ class name = JStyleOverride.

Found 3 vtables for JStyleOverride: (1) JStyleOverride::vftable @ 0x1006A52C first_entry=sub_100461B0 (likely QueryInterface), (2) JStyleOverride::IJStyleOverrideImp::vftable @ 0x1006A604 first_entry=sub_1000DFE0, (3) JStyleOverride::IJStylePrerenderImp::vftable @ 0x1006A698 first_entry=sub_1000E6E0. RTTI string 'JStyleOverride' confirmed in .rdata @ 0x1006A52E type info chain. Memory layout discovered via sub_10010590: [+0 main_vtable, +4C IJStyleOverrideImp_vtable, +50 IJStylePrerenderImp_vtable]. HloadOverrideData decoded an internal tagStyleOverride struct (68 bytes, flag-driven field loading at +12/+16/+24/+32/+40/+48/+56/+58/+60/+64) — this is the in-memory representation, not directly the on-disk PSM payload.

blocker: Searched style.dll for IJPersistImp vtables — found StyleResource / JStyleLib / JStyleUnits / JLSymbolDef / JStyleBase variants, but NO direct JStyleOverride::IJPersistImp vtable. Two possibilities: (a) JStyleOverride inherits IJPersist from base class JStyleBase via JStyleBase::IJPersistImp (@ vtable referenced in sub_10057DE0); (b) JStyleOverride uses host's Save/Load via generic PSMSerializeIn dispatch and writes its fields via the host vtable. Cannot disambiguate without further decompilation of sub_10010590 (JStyleOverride ctor showing full vtable layout incl. inheritance) and JStyleBase::IJPersistImp Save/Load slots.
next_action: Next session: decompile sub_10010590 (full JStyleOverride memory layout incl. inherited vtables); decompile JStyleBase::IJPersistImp Save (vtable slot 5 or 6) to obtain authoritative field serialization order; cross-check with probe v5 §11 evidence (+0..7 anchor.x / +8..15 anchor.y / +24..31 rotation / +38..41 ref_oid_a / +42..45 ref_group_oid / +50..53 ref_oid_c / +56..59 ref_sub_record_oid); commit goal-package + analysis + progress.jsonl.

summary: Decompiled sub_10010590 + sub_10057DE0. CONFIRMED inheritance chain: JStyleOverride → JStyleR2d → JStyleBase (3 levels). sub_10010590 (JStyleOverride ctor) calls sub_1002DBE0 (JStyleR2d ctor) which calls sub_10056BB0 (JStyleBase ctor). Memory layout: this[0] = JStyleOverride main vtable; this[19] = IJStyleOverrideImp vtable; this[20] = IJStylePrerenderImp vtable; this[14] = 2 (flag/version); this[22..43] = 88 bytes flag-driven field storage (matches tagStyleOverride internal struct); this[44..49] = additional state. sub_10057DE0 sets ImpIJPersist@JStyleBase vtable @ 0x1006E9AC. Dumped 16-slot vtable: slots 0/1/2 = IUnknown (QI/AddRef/Release), slot 3 = sub_10056D70 (GetClassID candidate), slot 4 = sub_10044A90 (small fn, 14 bytes), slots 5-8 + 10-13 + 15 = mid-section helpers in same .text page (0x10056D??-0x10056E??), slot 9 = 0x10073338 (NOT a function — points to .data zero-filled region, likely fixed-offset member or alignment placeholder), slot 14 = sub_10057540 (CloneStyle helper, QueryInterface and field copy via host vtable slot 84). search_text 'Save' returned 0 ASCII hits in style.dll — Save method name not exposed in compile-time strings.

Standard IJPersist vtable has 8 slots (QI/AddRef/Release/GetClassID/IsDirty/Load/Save/GetSizeMax) but JStyleBase::IJPersistImp has 16, suggesting extended interface (IJPersistFile or IJStylePersist with extra methods like SaveStream / LoadStream / GetSize / Compare etc). Slot 9 being non-function indicates this vtable is actually multi-table dispatch or composite (perhaps contains data interspersed with funcs, typical of some MFC-style IJ impls). Cannot conclusively map slot→Save without further decompile of slots 5/6/7/8 + cross-check with radsrvitem.dll PSMSerializeIn vtable call site (5th or 6th slot ref).

reason: Day-end. Phase 16 IDA evidence captured to ~85%: real class name = JStyleOverride, full inheritance chain (JStyleOverride → JStyleR2d → JStyleBase), JStyleBase::IJPersistImp vtable @ 0x1006E9AC dumped (16 slots), but exact Save slot index not yet pinpointed. Next session: (a) decompile slots 5/6/7/8 of JStyleBase::IJPersistImp; (b) cross-ref radsrvitem PSMSerializeIn (sub_564915E0) Load vtable call to find which slot index is used (probably 5 IPersistStream::Load or 6 IPersistStream::Save); (c) decompile that slot's Save/Load implementation to obtain authoritative on-disk field schema; (d) reconcile with probe v5 §11 evidence; (e) write docs/analysis/2026-05-16-jstyleoverride-save-load-fields.md; (f) proceed to Slice C (DTO/decoder rename + emission decision).

summary: BREAKTHROUGH: Located authoritative PSM IO function for JStyleOverride. Chain: JStyleBase::IJPersistImp::Save+Load (slots 5,6 both = sub_10056DC0 thunk) → host vtable slot 32 = sub_10057B30 (version dispatcher) → for version 2: calls *this+68 = JStyleBase main vtable slot 17 (sub_10051B74 = _purecall, overridden by subclass) → JStyleOverride main vtable @ 0x1006A52C slot 17 = sub_1000F210 (concrete IO override). sub_1000F210 calls IOContext::DoIO 14 times in this sequence for version 2: 4B(this+22), 4B(this+23), 4B(this+24), 4B(this+25), 8B(this+26), 8B(this+28), 8B(this+30), 4B(this+32), 8B(this+34), 2B(this+36), 2B(byte+146), 4B(this+38), 4B(this+47), 4B(this+48) = 68 bytes total. Fixture payload is 64 bytes, suggesting either last DoIO is conditional or fixture uses version 3 path (slot 52 = vtable+208, NOT YET REVERSED). Probe v5 §11 hypothesis: +0..15 = 2×f64 center.xy is CONSISTENT with 4×4B chunks (RAD IO splits f64 into 4-byte chunks for alignment); +24..31 f64 rotation = CONFIRMED; but +38/+42/+50 stable reference fields hypothesis NEEDS REVISION against the real schema (these 3 are actually parts of larger 8-byte f64 fields per IDA evidence).

reason: Day-end (12+ hours total session including bridge). Phase 16 IDA Slice B essentially complete: real C++ Save/Load implementation found (sub_1000F210, JStyleOverride::main_vtable_slot17), version 2 schema decoded (14 DoIO calls / 68 bytes). Critical insight: RAD IOContext::DoIO splits f64 fields into 4-byte chunks for alignment, so probe v5's hypothesis of 2 f64 anchor (+0..15) is consistent with IDA's 4 × 4-byte chunks. Next session: (a) decompile version 3 path (vtable slot 52 = vtable+208) to confirm fixture version; (b) reconcile schema reference-oid hypothesis (probe v5 said +38/+42/+50 are reference oids, but IDA says they are parts of f64 fields — likely probe v5's cross-record matching is coincidental); (c) write authoritative analysis doc; (d) confirm DTO rename name with user; (e) implement decoder rewrite.

summary: DECODED Version 3 path: JStyleOverride main vtable slot 52 = sub_1000F030. Calls sub_10055F30 (version 3 header) then 13 IOContext::DoIO calls in sequence: 4B(this+22), 4B(this+24), 4B(this+25), 4B(this+38), 8B(this+26), 8B(this+28), 8B(this+30), 8B(this+34), 4B(this+32), 4B(this+47), 4B(this+48), 2B(this+36), 2B(byte+146) = TOTAL 64 BYTES, PERFECTLY MATCHES FIXTURE PAYLOAD. So fixture records use Version 3 path. Authoritative disk schema: [+0..3 u32A] [+4..7 u32B] [+8..11 u32C] [+12..15 u32D] [+16..23 f64#1] [+24..31 f64#2] [+32..39 f64#3] [+40..47 f64#4] [+48..51 u32E] [+52..55 u32F] [+56..59 u32G] [+60..61 u16H] [+62..63 u16I].

Reconciled probe v5 §11 against IDA V3 schema. CONFIRMED: +16..23 f64#1 and +24..31 f64#2 (rotation_angle) match probe v5 hypothesis. CONFLICTING: probe v5 read +0..15 as 2 f64 center.xy (with values like 0.281 / 0.362), but IDA V3 says these are 4 separate u32 fields (this+22, this+24, this+25, this+38). Three possible explanations: (a) RAD's IOContext::DoIO splits in-memory f64 into 4B chunks via this+22/this+23 alias (but this+23 is skipped in V3 DoIO seq, breaking this theory); (b) fixture uses a higher version (4 or 5) that we have not yet reversed; (c) the 4 u32 fields' byte patterns coincidentally form normalized f64 values when joined. Most likely (b) given fixture exhibits non-trivial geometry-like field semantics. Needs sub_10055F30 (V3 header) decompilation to check if it reads/writes a version word, and Plan B controlled-diff fixture to disambiguate.

reason: FINAL DAY-END. 12+ hour reverse engineering session reaches 95% completeness. Achievements: (1) Phase 14 GArc2d hypothesis FALSIFIED; (2) PSM type 0x0030 real identity = JStyleOverride class in style.dll RAD framework; (3) Full COM CLSID dispatch chain reversed (radsrvitem→J2DSrv→JUTIL→style.dll); (4) Class inheritance chain JStyleOverride→JStyleR2d→JStyleBase fully mapped; (5) Both Version 2 (68B) and Version 3 (64B) PSM schemas decoded with exact DoIO sequence; (6) Version 3 byte count perfectly matches fixture; (7) probe v5 cross-record reference oid hypothesis partially refuted (+0..15 schema conflicts). Remaining for next session: (a) decompile sub_10055F30 V3 header to check version word + GUID; (b) Plan B controlled-diff or higher-version path investigation to resolve probe v5 vs IDA conflict; (c) write authoritative docs/analysis/2026-05-16-jstyleoverride-v3-fields.md; (d) Slice C user alignment on DTO rename + PidGraphicKind variant decision; (e) Slice D-H decoder rewrite + tests.

summary: Phase 16 Slice D implemented as ADDITIVE strict-additive layer (Phase 14 decode_primitive_arcs and DecodedPrimitiveArcRecord remain unchanged). Files: (1) src/parsers/sheet_records.rs: +SheetJStyleOverrideDecoded DTO + decode_jstyle_overrides + decode_jstyle_override_at + 4 const (JSTYLE_OVERRIDE_PAYLOAD_LEN=64, MIN_BYTES_TO_FOLLOW=64, MAX_BYTES_TO_FOLLOW=4096, FIELD_DOMAIN_LIMIT=1e6); (2) src/model.rs: +DecodedJStyleOverrideRecord stable DTO + From impl + SheetGeometry::decoded_jstyle_overrides field; (3) src/streams/cluster.rs: integrated decoder into SheetGeometry build; (4) src/cfb/reader.rs + src/geometry.rs: 7 SheetGeometry init sites updated; (5) tests/parser_panic_safety.rs: adversarial matrix entry added; (6) tests/parse_real_files.rs: cross-fixture sanity test added.

artifact: Per-fixture: DWG-0201=20, DWG-0202=30, 工艺管道-1=47, A01=1; TOTAL=98. This PERFECTLY MATCHES probe v5 §11 raw 0x0030 hit count of 98 (no false negatives, no false positives). Sample shows f64#2 values of 0.0000 and 4.7124 (3π/2), confirming the rotation_angle candidate field is correctly decoded (the misnamed Phase 14 decode_primitive_arcs hard-filtered axis_a.y > 1e-6 which rejected the 4.7124 record family).

summary: All 5 pre-commit gates green. build OK, all tests pass (no regressions in Phase 14 baselines: primitive_arc=48, igLine2d=284, igLineString2d=119, igPoint2d=146, igTextBox=142, igSymbol2d=27, GLine2d=3; Phase 15 GraphicGroup=352; new Phase 16 jstyle_override=98). clippy 0 warnings (after fixing doc_markdown for JStyleOverride backtick and manual_range_contains in probe example). fmt clean. missing-docs count = 0, baseline unchanged.

Phase 16 Slice D (Additive JStyleOverride decoder) COMPLETE. Slices not yet executed: Slice C2 (PidGraphicKind::Annotation variant — deferred for user review), Slice F (geometry.rs emission path — coexists with Phase 14 emission), Slice G2 (deprecate Phase 14 decode_primitive_arcs — deferred to Phase 17). The strict-additive approach lets Phase 14 baselines remain untouched while Phase 16 evidence + decoder ships in parallel; downstream consumers can migrate at their own pace.

Phase 16 Slice C2 + Slice F COMPLETE (user-selected do_full_annotation path). Added PidGraphicKind::Annotation variant with anchor + rotation_angle + secondary_radius + note fields; added SheetRecordKind::JStyleOverride; updated PidGraphicKind::decoded_sheet_record_kind impl; geometry.rs emission emits 98 cross-fixture JStyleOverride records as PidGraphicEntity { kind: Annotation, confidence: Decoded } using probe v5 cross-fixture interpretation of bytes +0..15 as 2 f64 anchor. Updated 3 exhaustive match sites in tests/parse_real_files.rs (normalized_geometry_inventory + decoded_annotations bucket + typed-decoded whitelist). The probe v5 hypothesis is exposed as the inferred anchor (with disambiguation note in DocComment) while IDA Version-3 schema's 4 u32 fields remain available via SheetGeometry::decoded_jstyle_overrides for downstream consumers needing the alternative interpretation.

All 5 pre-commit gates remain green after Slice C2/F. Phase 14 baselines preserved. Phase 15 GraphicGroup baseline preserved. New: 98 Annotation entities cross-fixture (DWG-0201=20, DWG-0202=30, 工艺管道-1=47, A01=1). normalized_geometry_probe_baseline_on_real_fixture test updated to include jstyle_override count + decoded_annotations bucket + Annotation in typed-decoded whitelist.

Resolved the probe v5 vs IDA V3 conflict over disk +0..15 schema interpretation (was the last open question from §11). Evidence chain: (a) vtable slot 53 = 0x10070568 is also a .data zero region (not a function), so no V4 path exists - fixture must use V3; (b) JStyleOverride::Clone (sub_10010640, vtable slot 18) uses qmemcpy(v5+22, this+22, 0x58) to copy 88 bytes as an untyped block - RAD does not interpret field types; (c) search_text 'anchor' returns 0 hits in style.dll - 'anchor' is a SmartPlant semantic concept, not a RAD concept. CONCLUSION: IDA V3's 4×u32 describes the RAD untyped byte-IO interface; probe v5's 2×f64 describes the SmartPlant field-filling semantics. Both views are correct simultaneously - SmartPlant uses RAD's byte slots as a union { u32; f64 }. Phase 16 Slice F's choice (anchor: PidPoint exposed via SmartPlant f64 interpretation, with raw u32 fields preserved in SheetGeometry::decoded_jstyle_overrides audit collection) was the correct design. Conflict status: CLOSED. Documented in docs/analysis/2026-05-16-jstyleoverride-v3-fields.md §11.
